title: Finance Executive/ Finance Clerk
description:
Job Responsibilities

1. Payroll & CPF Computation and Scheduling
- Responsible for accurately calculating monthly employee payroll, including basic salary, allowances, overtime, and deductions (such as unpaid leave, salary advances, etc.).
- Must be familiar with Singapore Central Provident Fund (CPF) contribution regulations, including both employee and employer contribution rates.
- Ensure accurate CPF calculations are reflected in the payroll and that CPF submissions are completed on time each month.
- Prepare detailed payroll schedules to support bank salary payments or cheque issuance.

2. Bank Payment Entry & Expense Reimbursement Processing
- Responsible for executing various payments through banking platforms (e.g. FAST, GIRO, telegraphic transfers).
- Accurately input approved payment documents (such as supplier invoices and expense claims) into the online banking system. Perform dual verification to ensure payee details, amounts, and payment references are correct.
- Review employee expense claims to ensure compliance with company policies, proper approvals, and complete supporting documents before arranging payments.

3. Daily Expense Payments
- Handle petty cash expenditures and urgent payment requirements.
- Manage petty cash for small operational expenses such as courier fees or emergency purchases, maintaining accurate petty cash records.
- Ensure timely payment of utilities, internet, and other operational bills to avoid late charges.

4. Additional Responsibilities (Typically Included)
- Perform daily cash counts and manage bank deposits and withdrawals.
- Conduct regular bank reconciliations to ensure company records match bank statements and resolve outstanding items.
- Monitor bank account balances to ensure sufficient funds.
- Accurately and timely record all cash and bank transactions in the accounting system or cash journals.
- Systematically organize and archive all payment vouchers, bank advices, and supporting documents to ensure proper audit trails.

5. Compliance & Reporting
- Assist in preparing documents required for annual audits.
- Ensure compliance with Singapore financial, tax, and employment regulations (in addition to CPF, may include Skills Development Levy, etc.).
- Assist in preparing basic cash flow reports.

⸻

Job Requirements:

Knowledge & Skills
• Basic accounting knowledge and familiarity with local banking procedures.
• Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other office applications.
• Knowledge of Singapore CPF regulations and relevant employment laws is a strong advantage.

Personal Attributes
• High level of integrity, reliability, and attention to detail.
• Strong numerical accuracy and organizational skills.
• Ability to manage time effectively and meet recurring deadlines such as monthly payroll cycles.
• Basic communication skills.

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
